reason_for_decision: The article passes because it separates first notice from maintained state clearly and translates that distinction into concrete system requirements.
strengths: The article keeps the detection-versus-tracking distinction crisp from the opening paragraph onward.
It identifies update cadence, association logic, and continuity as the real engineering shift once tracking is required.
The piece is especially useful for preventing teams from mistaking alert capability for stable track capability.
summary_assessment: This is a clear pass under the strict rubric. It has good technical discipline and strong decision value for readers evaluating low-altitude monitoring systems.
